subagent_reports.topic: Validate adding a required relevance-validation step before /adv-triage Phase 4b field-assignment prompts

sources.label: .opencode/command/adv-triage.md
sources.summary: Phase 3 Match+Identify Gaps builds represented[]/unrepresented[]; Phase 4b builds field-assignment matrix (bug priority labels, feature Value) and prompts directly via question tool. No relevance/staleness check exists; matrix gate is only 'matrix non-empty'. Confirms the design's premise: stale/already-addressed open issues flow straight into Phase 4b scoring prompts.

sources.label: skills/adv-triage/WSJF.md
sources.summary: Match algorithm = stable-ref/body-excerpt/title-similarity for DEDUP against open issues, not relevance. User field-assignment matrix table (lines 52-59) keys on 'issue has bug, no priority label' / 'issue has feature, Value null' — purely field-presence, no obsolescence/already-fixed filter.

sources.label: skills/adv-triage/PROMPTS.md
sources.summary: User-only field assignments section (lines 50-86): Stage 1 batch control options are One-by-one/Autofill/Defer-all/Stop; Stage 2 bug priority + feature Value options. 'Defer' exists per-item but no upstream prompt asks whether item is still relevant/already-addressed before scoring.

sources.label: skills/adv-triage/ANTI-PATTERNS.md + SKILL.md
sources.summary: grep for relevance|stale|already.address|still relevant|obsolete across skills/adv-triage returned zero matches. SKILL.md Core Flow step 5 (User fields) and Structural Rules confirm no relevance gate. Confirms feature is genuinely net-new, not a duplication of existing behavior.

sources.label: plugin/src/adv-improve-assets.test.ts
sources.summary: Canonical asset-test pattern: REPO_ROOT=resolve(__dirname,'../..'), join COMMAND_PATH/skill path, readFileSync + content.toContain/.toMatch assertions on command + skill markdown. This is the by-the-book template for the proposed plugin/src asset test; ~37 *-assets.test.ts files follow it.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Existing pattern: /adv-triage Phase 3 deduplicates inventory against open issues (structural ref/body/title match) but never validates whether a matched/open item is still RELEVANT (not stale, not already-fixed) before Phase 4b drives it into user scoring prompts. The reported bug (#133/#141/#145 scored despite being addressed) is a real gap, not user error. Reference pattern: backlog hygiene best practice separates (a) dedup, (b) relevance/triage-out of obsolete items, (c) prioritization scoring — relevance gating precedes scoring so users never spend judgment on dead items. The proposed insertion of a required relevance-validation step BEFORE Phase 4b prompts aligns with that ordering and is the boring/correct fix. Deviation: MINOR — additive gate, no structural rework. Design choices are sound: (1) command owns orchestration/gate, skill owns rubric/prompt template (matches SKILL.md line 15 canonical-source contract); (2) asset test under plugin/src matches the established *-assets.test.ts convention. Note: PROMPTS.md is the better skill home than WSJF.md for a new relevance PROMPT (PROMPTS.md owns question-tool prompt templates; WSJF.md owns scoring math) — recommend prompt template in PROMPTS.md, any relevance rubric/criteria in WSJF.md or SKILL.md Structural Rules.

validation.notes: Design is VALIDATED with non-blocking refinements. Premise verified against source: no existing relevance gate; #133/#141/#145 class of problem is real. Risks are scope/placement, not feasibility.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED. Implement as: (1) Insert Phase 4-relevance step in adv-triage.md BEFORE 4b field-assignment matrix — gate each candidate (matched-open + unrepresented bound for scoring) through relevance check; outcomes = relevant / stale-close / already-addressed-close / defer. Use structural signals first (P33): linked-change archived/closed, issue already closed, referenced file/symbol gone, superseding issue. AI relevance judgment is advisory only and must route to a user question, never auto-close. (2) Add relevance PROMPT template to PROMPTS.md (question tool, not plain chat — consistent with existing 4b rule); put relevance criteria/rubric in WSJF.md or SKILL.md Structural Rules. (3) Add a Constraint line ('MUST NOT prompt for Priority/Value before relevance validation') and an ANTI-PATTERNS row. (4) Asset test plugin/src/adv-triage-relevance-assets.test.ts modeled on adv-improve-assets.test.ts.
follow_ups: Decide exact close semantics: does relevance-validation actually `gh issue close` obsolete items, or only exclude-from-scoring + surface for user close? Closing is a mutation → must be Tier B approval-gated like issue creation. Design should state this explicitly.

Confirm whether relevance check applies only to feature-Value/bug-priority candidates or to ALL open issues in scope (broader scan = more value but more API/budget cost; ties into existing GraphQL budget gate).

TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION packet anchors were present in prompt; SCOPE KEY/ATTEMPT were inferred from defaults — orchestrator should confirm scope_key/attempt if exact provenance matters.

documents.proposal:
# Update triage relevance checks

## Problem

`/adv-triage` currently builds the bug Priority / feature Value assignment matrix directly from open issues and project field gaps. During the latest triage run, user prioritization prompts exposed stale/already-addressed items (#133, #141, #145) that should have been relevance-checked before asking for user-owned scores.

## Direction

Make relevance validation a required step before Phase 4b field prompts. The agent must inspect available evidence for each missing-field item — issue state/body/comments, linked ADV change state, current source/docs when the item claims an implementation gap — and remove/close/defer stale or already-satisfied items before prompting for Priority/Value.

## Success Criteria

- `/adv-triage` command contract requires relevance validation before Phase 4b `question` prompts.
- `adv-triage` skill prompt/scoring guidance says stale/already-addressed items must be investigated and resolved before asking user to score.
- Existing hard rules remain intact: bug priority and feature Value are user-owned; no autonomous scoring of those fields for active relevant items.
- Asset/contract tests cover the new relevance-check requirement so it cannot silently regress.

## Scope

- `.opencode/command/adv-triage.md`
- `skills/adv-triage/*.md`
- Relevant asset tests under `plugin/src/`

## Out of Scope

- Rebuilding triage implementation as a separate tool.
- Changing GH Project schema or WSJF formula.
documents.problemStatement: Triage asked the user to prioritize/value stale or already-addressed backlog items. The workflow should relevance-check field-gap candidates before user scoring prompts, so the user is not forced to classify items that should be cancelled, removed, or deferred after evidence review.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Add an explicit relevance-validation step before `/adv-triage` asks for bug Priority or feature Value.
2. Preserve user ownership of bug Priority and feature Value for relevant active items.
3. Ensure stale/already-addressed items can be closed, removed, or deferred before user scoring.
4. Lock the behavior with asset/contract tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `.opencode/command/adv-triage.md` states Phase 4b must relevance-check field-gap candidates before `question` prompts.
2. `skills/adv-triage/PROMPTS.md` or `WSJF.md` defines evidence sources and outcomes for relevance validation.
3. A repo test fails if the command/skill no longer contain the relevance-check-before-prioritization requirement.
4. `bin/oc-test targeted -- <test file>` or equivalent targeted verification passes.

## Constraints

- Do not let heuristics close or suppress items without user approval where destructive action is involved.
- Do not auto-assign bug Priority.
- Do not auto-assign feature Value unless user explicitly selects autofill.
documents.design:
# Design

- Treat relevance validation as a Phase 4b pre-step, after issue creation/project addition and before the user field assignment matrix is prompted.
- Evidence hierarchy: open issue body/comments/labels/project status, linked ADV change state, current source/docs/tests for implementation-gap claims, and user-provided context.
- Outcomes: include in scoring prompt, close/remove with user approval, defer with reason, or update/link to represented work.
- Add a concise asset test near existing command/skill asset coverage that asserts command and skill text contain the required relevance-check-before-field-prompt anchors.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

`/adv-triage` now requires relevance validation before asking the user for bug Priority or feature Value. Field-gap candidates must be checked against issue evidence, linked ADV change state, and current source/docs/tests when applicable; stale, already-addressed, duplicate, or unclear items are resolved before scoring prompts.

The command contract, triage skill prompts, and anti-pattern guidance were updated. A new asset test locks the relevance-validation-before-field-assignment requirement and verifies the documented outcomes. Targeted verification passed via `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/adv-triage-relevance-assets.test.ts`.

Reviewed at: 2026-06-06T20:03:15.994Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | `.opencode/command/adv-triage.md` states Phase 4b must relevance-check field-gap candidates before `question` prompts. | pass | .opencode/command/adv-triage.md contains `### 4b. Relevance validation` before `### 4c. Field assignments` and MUST NOT prompt Priority/Value before relevance validation. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | `skills/adv-triage/PROMPTS.md` or `WSJF.md` defines evidence sources and outcomes for relevance validation. | pass | skills/adv-triage/PROMPTS.md contains Relevance validation with evidence sources and outcomes: relevant, stale/already-addressed, duplicate/superseded, unclear. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | A repo test fails if the command/skill no longer contain the relevance-check-before-prioritization requirement. | pass | `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/adv-triage-relevance-assets.test.ts` passed after reviewer remediation. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | `bin/oc-test targeted -- <test file>` or equivalent targeted verification passes. | pass | Asset test asserts command and skill anchors; command constraints still forbid autofill bug priority and autonomous feature Value unless user chooses autofill. |
| C1 | constraint | Do not let heuristics close or suppress items without user approval where destructive action is involved. | respected | Command and PROMPTS state relevance heuristics are advisory only and require explicit user approval before close/remove/defer/suppress. |
| C2 | constraint | Do not auto-assign bug Priority. | respected | Command constraints retain `MUST NOT autofill bug priority` and bugs use priority labels only. |
| C3 | constraint | Do not auto-assign feature Value unless user explicitly selects autofill. | respected | PROMPTS preserves Value as user-owned unless user explicitly selects autofill. |
